name: Hiawatha National Bank
description_short: FDIC-insured community bank offering personal and business checking, savings, mortgages, and digital banking services across multiple Midwest locations.
description_long: Hiawatha National Bank (HNB) is a traditional community bank operating in the Midwest with physical branch locations in Hager City, Glenwood City, Maiden Rock, and Hudson, Wisconsin. The bank is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to federal limits. The bank offers comprehensive personal banking services including checking and savings accounts, debit/ATM cards with control features, direct deposit, mobile and internet banking, bill pay (H-Pay), and mortgage products for purchase, refinancing, and construction. For business customers, HNB provides cash management tools, remote deposit capabilities, night drop services with locked bags, and business checking accounts. All customers have access to ATM networks with refund programs, mobile deposit via smartphone, and a 4:00 PM cutoff for same-day processing eligibility. Hiawatha National Bank distinguishes itself through personalized service, assigning every client a personal banker as their primary contact. The bank emphasizes customer security with proactive fraud alerts (including warnings about Nacha scam calls), debit card control applications allowing real-time transaction monitoring and merchant-specific restrictions, and 24/7 card support. Safe deposit boxes are available at select locations for document and valuables storage. As a traditional community bank, HNB serves customers seeking local relationship banking with FDIC protection and digital convenience. However, the bank operates only in Wisconsin with limited branch locations, and the website provides minimal information about interest rates, account minimums, fees, or competitive product terms—typical limitations for community banks with smaller marketing budgets.
services: Business checking accounts with cash management
Mobile banking app with mobile deposit
Internet banking and online account access
Debit/VISA cards with Card Control app
Night drop service with locked bags
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full federal backing
Personalized banking with assigned personal banker for each client
ATM refund program allowing fee-free cash access at any ATM
Debit Card Control app enables real-time transaction alerts and merchant-specific spending limits
Mobile deposit and remote deposit capabilities for both personal and business customers
4:00 PM cutoff time for next-business-day fund availability on deposits
Night drop service with locked bags available for business customers
cons: Limited geographic footprint restricted to Wisconsin (four known branch locations)
Website lacks transparent information on interest rates, APYs, and fee structures
No mention of loan products beyond mortgages for personal customers
Minimal digital innovation marketing compared to neobanks and larger regional competitors
Limited online account opening or enrollment process information visible on website
best_for: Wisconsin residents seeking FDIC-insured community banking with personalized service
Small business owners needing cash management, remote deposit, and local relationship banking
Customers prioritizing fraud protection and real-time debit card control features
diagnosis: Hiawatha National Bank is best for Wisconsin residents and small business owners seeking traditional community banking with FDIC insurance, personalized service, and strong fraud protection features. The main caveat is geographic limitation to four Wisconsin locations and lack of transparent pricing information on the website, making it essential for prospective customers to contact the bank directly at (888) 460-8723 or clientservices@hnbank.com for specific rates and fees.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
